title: 🎙️ Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling
description: Local arts scene, festivals, creative economy, media, and cultural tech - Daily themed article selection for podcast generation

_podcast.theme_description: Local arts scene, festivals, creative economy, media, and cultural tech
_podcast.theme_scoring_prompt: Rate how well this article fits the theme 'Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling' through a RURAL TECH AND INNOVATION lens. Strong fits include: digital storytelling platforms and tools, community media tech (podcasting, streaming, local publishing), creative tech economy in small towns, rural cultural sector innovation, open-source arts tools, Cariboo arts festivals using digital outreach, preservation of rural cultural heritage through technology, animation/film/media production in rural BC, local radio/podcast infrastructure, and how rural communities use technology for creative expression. Prefer articles that bridge culture AND technology in rural/Cariboo contexts. Score 0-100 where 100 is a perfect thematic match and 0 is completely unrelated.

items.title: [Make Magazine] Desktop Pixel Art Player p3a
items.content_html:
p3a is a Wi-Fi pixel art player built on the ESP32-P4. It is a 4-inch smart art frame that plays animated artworks ("gifs") directly from the internet or from local SD card. It is a playful decorative item and a fun desktop companion.

The post Desktop Pixel Art Player p3a appeared first on Make: DIY Projects and Ideas for Makers.

items.title: [New Atlas] Off-grid micro house fits everything you need into just 215 sq ft
items.content_html:
This off-grid cabin-style tiny house is seriously small, with a floorspace of just 20 sq m (215 sq ft). However, despite its compact dimensions, it has been expertly designed to provide everything needed for a short stay.

items.title: [Boing Boing] Why you can't sleep the first night in a new place
items.content_html:
If you've ever traveled, you know that your first night in a new bed is often a sleepless one. That brain fog you wake up with feels like you've got five pounds of poop in a three-pound bag. The good news is there's a legitimate scientific explanation for why your brain won't shut down when you need it to. — Read the rest

The post Why you can't sleep the first night in a new place appeared first on Boing Boing.
